petitioner_background: The petitioner is self-represented and sought classification as an employment-based immigrant under section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Immigration and Nationality Act as an alien of extraordinary ability. No information about the petitioner's field of work, nationality, or career qualifications is provided in the decision text.
outcome_reasoning: The appeal was summarily dismissed because the petitioner failed to specifically identify any erroneous conclusion of law or statement of fact for the appeal, as required by 8 C.F.R. § 103.3(a)(1)(v). On appeal, the petitioner indicated a brief and/or evidence would be submitted within sixty days, but more than five months after the November 3, 2004 filing, the AAO had received nothing further. The petitioner did not address the reasons stated for denial and did not provide any additional evidence addressing the regulatory criteria at 8 C.F.R. § 204.5(h)(3).
legal_citations: Section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Immigration and Nationality Act
fraud_or_procedural_issues: Procedural dismissal: The petitioner failed to identify specifically any erroneous conclusion of law or statement of fact for the appeal. Although the petitioner indicated on appeal that a brief and/or evidence would be submitted within sixty days, no further submission was received more than five months after the appeal was filed on November 3, 2004, resulting in summary dismissal under 8 C.F.R. § 103.3(a)(1)(v).
